findingDetails.title: Phase-parent scope and lifecycle metadata omit a completed fifth child
findingDetails.evidence: The parent defines four lanes and maps phases 1-4, while child 005 is Complete and registered in graph-metadata.json.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile parent scope, phase map, status, and generated metadata with the five-child topology.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Final pass re-read parent spec, child metadata, and graph metadata.

findingDetails.title: Authored Markdown export is only provenance-marker checked
findingDetails.evidence: The writer validates the structured token brand but accepts any rendered Markdown containing origin: authored, while the template requires schema and value-level provenance.
findingDetails.recommendation: Validate rendered Markdown or render it from the validated structured brand.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Final pass compared the boundary implementation with the authored artifact template and checklist claims.

findingDetails.title: Paired authored exports are not refreshed atomically
findingDetails.evidence: Two independent writeFile calls run concurrently, so partial I/O failure can leave the paired exports inconsistent.
findingDetails.recommendation: Stage both files and commit the pair atomically or add rollback recovery.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Final pass re-read the paired-write implementation and export contract.

findingDetails.title: Authored leaf symlinks can redirect a permitted write into measured data
findingDetails.evidence: The writer checks lexical containment but calls writeFile on the final path without rejecting an existing symlink or using no-follow semantics.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reject symlinked destination entries with lstat and use no-follow or atomic final-write semantics.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Final pass re-read the boundary implementation, boundary spec, and adversarial tests.

results.summary: Parent lifecycle scope still disagrees with the registered five-child topology.
Recorded evidence does not cover the rendered-output contract, pair recovery, or final-path symlink redirection.
